Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a provided event log for a Procure to Pay P2P process I need to identify anomalies unusual or problematic behavior and explain why they are issues based on standard P2P practices

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Analyze the Data Structure Columns Case ID Timestamp Activity Cases 1001 1002 1003 1004

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Recall Standard P2P Flow The Happy Path Step 1 Need identified Create Purchase Requisition PR Step 2 Internal Control Approve Purchase Requisition Usually happens before ordering Step 3 External Commitment Issue Purchase Order PO Cannot issue PO without an approved PR Step 4 Physical Fulfillment Receive Goods Services Happens after PO is sent to vendor Step 5 Financial Claim Vendor Invoice Received Vendor sends bill after or upon delivery Step 6 Verification Match Invoice to PO 3 way match PO vs Goods Receipt vs Invoice Requires Invoice and Goods Receipt to exist Step 7 Execution Release Payment Only after matching approval of invoice Step 8 End Close Case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Sequence Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice Release Payment Close Case Analysis PR created then approved Yes PO issued after approval Yes Goods received after PO Yes Invoice received after goods Yes or close enough standard variance Match done before payment Yes Payment released last Yes Verdict This looks like the Golden Path or standard process No major anomalies here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Sequence Create PR Issue PO Anomaly Flag Issued before Approval Vendor Invoice Received Anomaly Flag Very early no goods received yet Release Payment Anomaly Flag Paid before goods received matched Approve Purchase Requisition Anomaly Flag Approved way too late after PO and Payment Receive Goods Anomaly Flag Goods arrived after payment Match Invoice to PO Anomaly Flag Matching happened after payment release Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Detailed Breakdown of Anomalies in 1002 Control Failure PO issued before PR approved This bypasses budget authorization Timing Approve PR happens on Feb 3rd but Issue PO was Feb 1st The approval is retroactive or just late Payment Risk Release Payment happened before Receive Goods and before Match Invoice to PO Paying before verifying goods were delivered is a huge fraud risk paying for nothing and accounting error risk Process Order Invoice received Payment Goods Received Match This is completely backwards

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Sequence Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Anomaly Flag Paid before receiving invoice goods Vendor Invoice Received Anomaly Flag Invoice arrives after payment Receive Goods Anomaly Flag Goods arrive after payment and after invoice recording Match Invoice to PO Anomaly Flag Matching at the very end Close Case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Detailed Breakdown of Anomalies in 1003 Prepayment Accrual Issue Maybe it is a prepayment But usually that is a specific activity type Create Prepayment Here it says Release Payment Releasing payment without a recorded invoice is generally bad practice unless explicitly defined as prepayment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Invoice Timing You cannot process payment properly if you have not received the invoice yet usually Or if you have why does the log say Invoice Received on Feb 9 when Payment Released was Feb 8 Goods Timing Paying on Feb 8 but not receiving goods until Feb 10 If this is not a prepayment the company paid for goods they did not have and did not have proof of delivery for yet Matching Matching happened last While sometimes matching lags doing it after payment release suggests the payment was not based on a verified match

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Sequence Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Anomaly Flag Goods received before PO issued Issue Purchase Order Anomaly Flag PO issued after receipt Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Detailed Breakdown of Anomalies in 1004 Maverick Buying Rush Order Receive Goods happened before Issue Purchase Order Usually you need a PO number to receive goods against in the system Receiving goods without a PO implies someone bought something outside the system or called the vendor directly without processing the requisition first This is often called Maverick buying Retroactive PO The PO is created after the fact to justify the receipt This undermines procurement controls

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Case 1001 Normal Case 1002 Severe control failures Payment before goods invoice PO before approval approval very late Case 1003 Potential prepayment or severe timing errors Payment before invoice receipt and goods receipt Case 1004 Maverick buying Retroactive PO Goods received before PO issued

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Drafting the Response Structure Introduction Case by Case Analysis Summary of Key Anomalies and Risks Tone Professional analytical business focused Refining Case 1002 Highlight the Rework or Late Approval aspect The fact that payment went out before goods were even seen is the biggest red flag here Refining Case 1003 Focus on the sequence Payment Invoice Goods This is highly irregular unless it is a specific prepayment scenario which is not labeled as such Refining Case 1004 Focus on the Goods before PO issue It indicates a lack of compliance with sourcing policies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Prompt Did I identify anomalies Yes Did I explain why based on P2P knowledge Yes Did I cover all cases Yes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure to distinguish between strict violation like paying before receiving vs inefficiency like slight timestamp overlaps Case 1002 is definitely a violation Case 1004 is a policy violation maverick buy
